profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-07-16 12:19:08 -0400.

did.abstract: Consists of the correspondence of Russian poet, translator, and writer Anatoly Naiman (1936-2022) along with some writings, photographs, personal documents, and clippings. Other individuals represented in the collection include Anna Ahkamatova, Joseph Brodsky, Sergei Dovlatov, Lidia Chukovskaya, Evgenii Rein, and Dmitrii Bobyshev.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection primarily consists of correspondence to Russian poet, translator, and writer Anatoly Naiman (1936-2022) from friends, family, and colleagues along with some writings, photographs, personal documents, and clippings. Other individuals represented in the collection include Anna Ahkamatova, Galina Narinskaya, and Naiman's mother, Asya Davidovna Naiman. Additional 20th-century Russian literary figures represented include Joseph Brodsky, Sergei Dovlatov, Lidia Chukovskaya, Evgenii Rein, and Dmitrii Bobyshev.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: A few commercial media types and publications were removed from the collection during 2023 processing.

Photocopies of items Naiman had requested from the Joseph Brodsky correspondence (MSS84477) collection at the Manuscript Division of the Library of Congress as well as digital system files and digital files with personally identifiable information were removed were removed during 2025-2026 processing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: While processors imposed intellectual organization for Naiman's papers as well as some physical organization of like materials, original order of physical arrangement at the folder level was largely maintained.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research with the exception of photocopies of letters from Naiman to Joseph Brodsky held at the Russian National Library, which are restricted for 10 years (through 2035) per the seller's stipulation.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Single copies may be made for research purposes. To cite or publish quotations that fall within Fair Use, as defined under U. S. Copyright Law, no permission is required. For instances beyond Fair Use, it is the responsibility of the researcher to determine whether any permissions related to copyright, privacy, publicity, or any other rights are necessary for their intended use of the Library's materials, and to obtain all required permissions from any existing rights holders, if they have not already done so. Princeton University Library's Special Collections does not charge any permission or use fees for the publication of images of materials from our collections, nor does it require researchers to obtain its permission for said use. The department does request that its collections be properly cited and images credited. More detailed information can be found on the Copyright, Credit and Citations Guidelines page on our website. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us through the Ask Us! form.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Anna Narinskaya in 2023 (AM 2024-021). Anna Narinskaya sent additional materials in 2024 (AM 2026-049).

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: For preservation reasons, original analog and digital media may not be read or played back in the reading room. Users may visually inspect physical media but may not remove it from its enclosure. All analog audiovisual media must be digitized to preservation-quality standards prior to use. Audiovisual digitization requests are processed by an approved third-party vendor. Please note, the transfer time required can be as little as several weeks to as long as several months and there may be financial costs associated with the process. Requests should be directed through the Ask Us Form.

This collection contains digital files, which may require specific software or hardware for access. Refer to our Tips on Accessing Born-Digital Content for information on how to render these file formats.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Faith Charlton and Thomas Keenan in 2023. Finding aid written by Faith Charlton and Thomas Keenan in 2023. Additional materials were processed by Faith Charlton and Thomas Keenan in 2025-2026. Finding aid written by Faith Charlton and Thomas Keenan in 2025-2026.

The born-digital materials in this collection have been processed according to Princeton University Library's Born-Digital Processing Workflows. For more information on the workflow, please read our full Born-Digital Processing Information Note.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Anatoly Naiman (1936-2022) was a Russian poet and translator who for a time served as secretary to fellow Russian poet, Anna Akhmatova. Along with Dmitrii Bobyshev, Joseph Brodsky, and Evgenii Rein, Anatoly Naiman was a member of the "Akhmatova Orphans" circle, a group of four poets whom Akhmatova called her "magic choir."

controlaccess.subject: Russian poetry. -- 20th century
Poets, Russian -- 21st century -- Sources
Russian poetry -- 21st century -- Sources
Literature and state -- Soviet Union. -- 20th century -- Sources
Translators in literature. -- Sources

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly correspondence to Naiman from family, friends, and colleagues along with some correspondence from Naiman. A good deal of correspondence is addressed to both Naiman and Galina Narinskaya and some to just Galina; some correspondence also involves other senders and recipients. Some of the correspondence includes manuscripts, typescripts, and photographs.
While most of the correspondence has been identified, there is a run of unidentified correspondence and related materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Per the seller's stipulation, photocopies of letters from Naiman to Joseph Brodsky (Box B-002306, Folder 47) held at the Russian National Library are restricted for 10 years (through 2035).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: received in an envelope labeled "Письма в Bryn Mawr"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes letters to Anna Narinskaya and Galina Narinskaya

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: letter or draft of letter to Dmitri Bobyshev?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: draft?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: letter addressed to "Raisa Grigor'evna" signed "Tolya, Galya, Anya, and Misha," i.e. Naiman, Galina Narinskaya, and their 2 children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: photocopies of correspondence from Naiman to Akhmatova; received in manilla envelope w/ inscription in pencil "Ф 1073, Ахматова А. А., N913. Найман, Анатолий Генрихович. Письма Ахматовой

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Naiman and others to Anna Akhmatova (and others?)

scopecontent.p: includes inserted color photographic prints

scopecontent.p: may also include letters to his brother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: includes inserted letters from others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: contains enclosed letters written by others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence addressed to Naiman's mother, Asya Davidnova Naiman, and father, Genrikh Naiman; inserts: homework assignment by young Anatoly Naiman?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: received with blank postcards depicting locales in Israel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes miscellaneous inserts, among them an apparent letter to Naiman's mother, Asya Davidnova Naiman, from someone else/ in another hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes one inserted undated letter possibly in another hand; one letter from Anna Narinskaya?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes letters from Naiman to his mother and father?; includes photographic prints and other miscellaneous inserts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in group labeled "Толя;" appears also to contain one letter in a different hand, including one from Liudmila Shtern

did.unittitle: Галина Михайловна Наринская (Galina Narinskaya) and Анна Анатольевна Наринская (Anna Narinskaya)?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence (letters, postcards, and telegrams) to to Efim Mikhailovich Slavinskii; found in envelope labeled "Славинский в тюрьме" (Slavinsky in prison) included, in folder

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Per the seller's stipulation, these letters are restricted for 10 years (through 2035).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most of the correspondence is to Naiman unless otherwise noted. A good deal is addressed to both Anatoly and Galina Naiman; some correspondence is neither to nor from Naiman- these are noted if known

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes letters from Amanda's Daughter "Becca," one informing Naiman of the death of Amanda

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes inserted photographic prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: correspondence to Naiman and Galina Narinskaya?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: correspondence to Naiman and Galina Narinskaya?; includes inserted letter from Leigh Caines; another letter signed Mirabeau?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence to Naiman from Mary Schwarz (ed. Of Yale Literary Magazine) and draft? handwritten letters from Naiman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes draft? handwritten letter from Naiman to Phillimore

did.unittitle: To Naiman and Galina Narinskaya from Various Correspondents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes a few letters from Naiman to others?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from George Kline; includes some slides

scopecontent.p: received in an envelope labeled "Письма в Bryn Mawr;" includes correspondence from George Kline, Tatyana Litvinova?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Svetlana Shtutina, Mira Meylakh, Faina Ranevskaya?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Svetlana Shtutina; includes photographic prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in group labeled "разное;" includes typescript of poem "Весёлая история;" includes letter from Naiman to "Fausto;" letter from Naiman to Narinskaya; unsigned, undated manuscript of prose text entitled "Осада;" set of pencil sketches (unsigned, undated); sundry other inserts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Igor Yefimov, Lev and/or Elena Poliakov, Masha Slonim, Liudmila Shtern, Tatiana Litvinova

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Oleg Tselkov, Masha Slonim?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Mira Meylac

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: some enclude enclosures (e.g. typescripts of poems, newspaper clippings, programs from poetry readings); includes letter from Naiman's mother, Asya Davidnova Naiman?; correspondence from Pamela Davidson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes correspondence from Arkadii Zastyrets, Naiman's mother?, Asya Davidnova Naiman; various enclosures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes letters from Igor Yefimov, (originals? Photocopies?) signed by Isaiah Berlin; letters from Tatiana Litvinova? "Таня Бутенева"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes photographic prints

did.unittitle: Станислав Яковлевич Красовицкий (Stanislav Krasovitsky) and Others
Anna Messina to Галина Михайловна Наринская (Galina Narinskaya)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters, postcards

did.unittitle: Владимир Аднреевич Скороденко (Vladimir Skorodenko) to Иосиф Александрович Бродский (Joseph Brodsky)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescript (with a few handwritten corrections) of a letter, including a contract with the publisher Художественная литература to publish a collection of Brodsky's poems; in envelope labeled in ink "Бродскому от Скороденко" (to Brodsky from Skorodenko)

did.unittitle: Minna to Галина Михайловна Наринская (Galina Narinskaya)?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: in group labeled "Минна"

did.unittitle: Вилен Лившиц (Vilen Livshits) to Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman)
To Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence to Naiman's mother from unidentified correspondents

did.unittitle: Letter to Anna Akhmatova from the Deputy Chief of Soviet Writers Union

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes Akhmatova can keep her room after her son's imprisonment; typescript on letterhead with autograph signature; typescript letter informing the Deputy General Secretary of the USSR Writers' Union B. Gorbatov of the head of the Main Directorate of the Northern Sea Route to allow Anna Akhmatova and her son to remain in their apartment

did.unittitle: Anna Akhmatova draft of letter to unknown addressee
From Anna Akhmatova to Kornei Chukovsky (typescript with autograph signature)
Telegram from Anna Akhmatova, Emma Gershtein and Sarra Arens to Anatoly Naiman on the Date of Joseph Brodsky's Release from the Exile

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: sent from Italy

scopecontent.p: Telegram from Anna Akhmatova to Anatoly Naiman from Komarovo to Estonia; handwritten by employee of the Soviet postal service?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1968 postcard from Joseph Brodsky addressed to Naiman, and one addressed to Brodsky apparently in Naiman's hand, signed "your Lo-li-ta"; 1989 handwritten postcard in Russian, signed in English "Entirely yours, J.B."; found in envelope labeled "Вольф. Бобышев. Рейн"

did.unittitle: Иосиф Александрович Бродский (Joseph Brodsky) to Galina Narinskaya

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Among the other letters, there is one greeting card (undated), where the signature is difficult to make out, but appears to be in the hand of Brodsky

did.unittitle: Biographical Sketch of Анна Андреевна Ахматова (Anna Akhmatova)

scopecontent.p: Typescript (6 pages) with heading "А.А.А. - Материалы А. Г. Наймана," first pages contain basic biographical information on Anna Akhmatova

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Playscript (typescript? mimeograph?) of "Evanna-Eva" by Naiman and Vasilii Alksenov, based on Aksenov's novel Вольтереьянцы и вольтерьянки (Voltairiens and Voltairiennes)

did.unittitle: Language on the Verge of a Nervous Breakdown Typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescript "Language on the Verge of a Nervous Breakdown" with handwritten marginalia; unidentified manuscript; clippings from unidentified publication

did.unittitle: Loving Anna Akhmatova: A Screenplay by Anatoly Naiman & Margo Rosen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: miscellaneous clippings, photocopies, and typescripts, including Naiman's "Витёк и Алик"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: miscellaneous manuscripts and typescripts; includes one set folded in half w/ front sheet labeled "Найман. Экспериментальная часть," appears to include manuscript material in different hands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple sheets of handwritten text

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: manuscripts, typescripts of drafts, notes; photocopies; loose pages from publications in green paper folder with label Leopardi/ Найман; some manuscripts, apparently in Naiman's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: manuscripts that appear to be in Naiman's hand (or at least most of it); in a rigid folder bearing on the front what appears to be a graphic image of the lamps on Trinity Bridge in St. Petersburg (Leningrad), and on the back bearing the embossed text "РПП З. Зак. 410 Тир.60000, 6-ХХ-70 г. Арт. ЛГ-097-01-379. Цена 90 коп. Изготовлено из отходов" (a series of acronyms/abbreviations and identifying numbers, indicating 60,000 units produced, price of 90 kopecs, and that it was made from scrap materials)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: unidentified handwritten text in notebooks, on cards; one postcard from "Рома;" some typescripts/photocopies with handwritten annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescripts of works by Naiman; mimeographs/ carbon copies?; miscellaneous manuscripts; originally in enclosure labeled, "Naiman. Early poems"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescripts and handwritten text (mostly verse) inside envelope

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to be in Naiman's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes poetry by Anna Akhmatova, Naiman, English translations of Naiman's poetry by George Kline

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook with dated handwritten entries, including prose and poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook with handwritten entries; one insert typescript; one inserted black and white photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: containing notes; drafts of correspondence; texts of poems, including "Как курильщик, кальяном сипя и дымя"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: notebook w/ title page "Форма номер один" (form or uniform numer one); many inserted loose pages w/ handwritten text, apparently in different hands, some postcards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: cardboard folder with manufacturer label "Папка для бумаг" (folder for papers), and manuscripts in ink "Анатолий Генрихович Найман/ 2163075/ Спасибо!/ Трубадуры/ второй экз." (Anatoly Genrikhovich Naiman/ 2163075/ Thank you!/ Troubadors/ second copy) containing manuscripts, typescripts of Russian translations of works by Bertran de Born, other manuscript pages, offprints/ loose pages with published texts, correspondence from the Sorbonne and Centre International de Documentation Occitane, L'Université Paul Valery (1980); Dr. phil. I. Ultich Gschwind (1981); postcard from 1990

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescript with handwritten annotations of poems

did.unittitle: Related to Анна Андреевна Ахматова (Anna Akhmatova)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: manuscripts and typescripts of texts received together in a folder: includes handwritten text of Boris Pasternak's poem "Improvisation" followed by the note "Борис Леонидович почему-то торопился непременно дописать этот новый вариант "Импровизации" - и пока Ахматова в Москве."; offprint or loose pages from a book; typescripts titled "Слово об Ахматовой/ из книги;" "Найман/ О поэзии Ахматовой;" "Н.Г. Чулкова/ Анна Ахматова;" envelope labeled "A.A.A." containing typescrips, handwritten notes, telegrams, handwritten note from Akhmatova?

did.unittitle: Related to Naiman's Translation of Troubadour Poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescripts, manuscripts, including drafts and some correspondence, most if not all related to Naiman's translation of troubadour verse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: manuscript and typescript translations of poetry by other poets, in a white card folder with a green and yellow front-cover design

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescripts of works, including "Сентябрьская поэма"; also included are greetings cards dated August 25 1979

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: miscellaneous typescripts (some with handwritten corrections, marginalia) photocopies, and manuscripts including, "Русская поэма: четыре опыта", "Akhmatova's Lessons," "The Status of Literature in Russia," "Великая душа," "Fall Notes upon Summer Impressions: A talk delivered at the WWC seminar in the Kennan Institute, 3. Dec. 1994," "A Jew in Two Cases"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 70 pages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Романс Розы Бутон (из телеспектакля слова А. Наймана, музыка Э. Артемьева (Romance of Rosa Buton from the television show "The Mystery of Erwin Drud," words by A. Naiman, music by E. Artem'ev); handwriting in ink on verso: "Сборник "Занавес поднят" песни и романсы из драматических спектаклей. Москва "Музыка" 1986 г." (Anthology "The Curtain's Up", songs and romances from shows. Moscow "Music" 1986)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of collection of Naiman's poems "Роща" (The Grove)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription on inside of folder: Anatolii Gerikhovich Naiman. Moscow. I -434. Dmitrovskoe shosse. d. 29 kv. 56

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of Naiman's collection of verse "Сентиментальный марш" (Sentimental march); original enclosure included handwritten label: "Найман А.Г., Л-д Ф-126, ул. Правды 12 кв. 50, [Н?]-47-21"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescripts/ offprints of poems - collection of verse Экстерриториальность - published in 2006 by the press "Новое издательство"; received in envelope labeled экстериториальность [sic.] - misspelling of title of one of Naiman's collections of verse: экстерриториальность

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: possibly galley proofs of Naiman's book of poetry that may not have been published

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: photocopies of typescripts and manuscript poetry, first page reading "Seeds of Silence. Amanda Haight;" accompanied by materials on a 1991 conference on Giacomo Leopardi ("VIII Convegno Internazionale Leopardiano Lingua e Stile di Giacomo Leopardi, Recanati, 30 settembre - 5 ottobre 1991")

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: miscellaneous manuscripts and typescripts; original folder labelled "Чужие стихи" (others' poems or other people's poems)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Akhmatova's translations of poetry by Giaccomo Leopardi, Akhmatova's own poetry, a manuscript with verse text signed by Amanda Haight, a publishing contract with the Государственное издательство художественной литературы

did.unittitle: Natalia Trauberg Translation of "The Grand Divorce"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescript, autograph inscription "Дорогим Гале и Толе с надеждой, радостью, благодарностью, и всем, что ещё бывает райского на земле. Наташа" [To Dear Galya and Tolya with hope, joy, gratitude, and all things celestial still encountered on earth. Natasha"]

did.unittitle: Margo Rosen Translations of Naiman Typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript translations of Naiman's verse by Margo Rosen, along with a related letter from Rosen to Naiman, dated 1998

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: handwritten writings in several small, numbered pages w/ numbered entries; found in envelope labeled "A. Найман/ Заметки корректора/ ЛЧ" and apparently in the hand of Lidia Chukovskaia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript essay by Roman Timenchik on Anna Akhmatova's play "Пролог" [Prologue], along with typescript draft of that play

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to include text from Valeria Sergeevna Sreznevskaia's remembrances of Akhmatova; smaller envelope labeled "To Anna Andreevna Akhmatova" A. Naiman?, on the inside 6 lines of handwritten verse text

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with handwritten insertions, marginalia, Anna Akhmatova "From the Cycle "Tashkent Pages"" (Из цикла "Ташкентские страницы")

did.unittitle: Anna Akhmatova: Пролог, или сон во сне

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript draft/transcript of Anna Akhamtova's poem "Пролог, или сон во сне" [Prologue, or Dream within A Dream]"

did.unittitle: Anna Akhmatova: Только тайно, чтоб никто не ведал

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Мanuscript (draft?) of poem by Anna Akhmatova ("Только тайно, чтоб никто не ведал")

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anna Akhmatova, typescript drafts of her essay on Amadeo Modigliani with autograph corrections/marginalia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: typescript of 2 poems with autograph corrections/marginalia: "Услаждала бредами" and "Кто чего боится"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with handwritten corrections/marginalia, Услаждала бредами, drafts of Anna Akmatova's prose/verse work "Листы из дневника" [Pages from a Diary]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of poem (Царскосельская ода) with handwritten corrections/marginalia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript of poem Предвесенняя элегия with handwritten corrections/marginalia and a miscellaneous work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript of Поэма без героя and footnotes for same with handwritten corrections/marginalia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with handwritten corrections/marginalia - a piece from the second part of Anna Akhmatova's Поэма без героя (Poem without A Hero)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph of poem "Шестая" (Услаждала бредами,/ Пением могил…)

did.unittitle: Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman) Notebooks
Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman) Notebooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 notebooks that appear to be in hand of Naiman's mother; notebook for 1984-1986 includes one insert postcard

did.unittitle: Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman) Notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: notebook that appears to be in hand of Naiman's mother; includes inserts such as handwritten and typescript manuscripts and postcards

did.unittitle: Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman) Notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: notebook/diary that appears to be in the hand of Naiman's mother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various writings and correspondence as well as an image of a 1959 letter from Anna Akhmatova. Some filenames include: Autobiog, Babel, Babette, Crisis.Lng, Diary, Galia, Jews, Letters, Story, Sultan, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of Naiman's laptop files including writings and correspondence among others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Naiman's Outlook files are restricted until they can be scanned for personally identifiable information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The folder titled "Uncategorized" was created during 2025 processing to organize loose files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes awards, diplomas, attestations, personal identification, family, and financial documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: from Октябрь: литературно-художественный журнал; The American Pushkin Academy of Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: a letter (undated) from the Leningrad Division of the USSR Writers Union attesting Naiman's participation in the 6th regional young writers conference; appraisal of Naiman's performance as a student of the Lensovet Technological Institute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: copies of birth certificates for Naiman and his son; diplomas; official correspondence; miscellaneous photocopies, typescripts, offprints, clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maxwell Micro Floppy Disk MF2.dd; handwritten on label "Naiman 94 (Wordperfect 5.1) CV"

did.unittitle: Diplomas and Certificates Granted to Naiman by the Journal Октябрь

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: copies of marriage certificates, birth certificates, divorce certificates, immigration documents, death certificate for Naiman's father (Genrikh Naiman), marriage license for Naiman's parents?, Savings account booklet, handwritten list of items given to Charles Isakovich Burdo in 1987

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 personal address and phone books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A majority of photographs are of Anna Akhmatova; most are black and white prints. There are some photographs scattered throughout the Correspondence series as noted in the description there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: mostly family photographs; some are postcards; mostly prints with some negatives from 1978

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 prints of the same photographic portrait in an envelope labeled "Е. С. Лeвитину" (to E. S. Levitin)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: color photographs of Joseph Brodsky with unidentified other man (Oleg Tselkov?), woman, apparently in New York City; black and white prints with text in pencil on verso, some featuring Akhmatova, one featuring Boris Pasternak?, Marina Tsvetaeva?; clipping of Ольшевский М. Анна Ахматова. Отношение к моей родине. - Советская молодежь, Рига, 1965, 3 июля; received in/ alongside envelope bearing ink inscription "Целков и др." followed by "Ахматовское и канон" crossed out

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 B&W photographic prints, featuring Akhmatova, Lev Gumilev

did.unittitle: Related to Анна Андреевна Ахматова (Anna Akhmatova)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with autograph inscription to Tatiana Litvinova on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with autograph inscription on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with autograph inscription to Naiman on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with autograph inscriptions on versos (second reads "Т. Наконец-то эта фотография… А. 10 июня 1964 Москва) [T. Finally, this photo. June 10 1964, Moscow]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: by Boris Shwartsman; 2 negatives with Shwartzman's signature on the enclosures

did.unittitle: Anna Akhmatova and Naiman in Budka, Komarova

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: taken by Lev Polyakov and signed by him on versos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: some inscribed by her

did.unittitle: Portrait of Фаина Георгиевна Раневская (Faina Ranevskaya)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with autograph inscription to Naiman on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: October 2004 issue of the journal "October." Featuring publication of manuscript of "unknown poem" by Anna Akhmatova with commentary by Naiman. Pages/ other material inserted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Наш Скопус, Commentary, Неприкосновенный запас, Linea d'Ombra, Носталгия: журнал для современников, Эксперт

did.unittitle: Clippings Concerning Анна Андреевна Ахматова (Anna Akhmatova's) Death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: pages from a 1915 issue of the journal Аполлон, containing the text of Akhamatova's poem "У самаго моря" ["Right by the sea"] with autograph inscription to Naiman (1965)

did.unittitle: Offprint of Anna Akhmatova Poems Published in Новый мир

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: w/ autograph marginalia and an inscription by Akhmatova to Naiman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Among other materials, includes: offprint or excerpt "Клюев и Есенин в октябре 1915 года (по материалам дневника Ф. Ф. Фидлера" Константин Азадовский, with inscription from the author to Naiman: "Дорогому Толе Найману в светлые дни февраля 87-го. К.А.); and offprint of a Novyi mir article by Maya Turovskaia, inscribed by the author to one "Levushka"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various correspondence, writings, photographs, and ephemera; some materials relate to Joseph Brodsky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous manuscripts, typescripts, photocopies, correspondence, offprints, loose pages from publications that were found in the same folder; includes typescript of "A Word on Akhmatova;" as well as letters from M. Adrov, Naiman's parents, and Amanda Haight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph labeled as taken in a Russian church in Claremont, Vermont; correspondence from: Naiman's mother, Ася Давидовна Найман (Asya Davidovna Naiman), Lidia Chukovskaya, Henry Hardy, George Kline; and a color, photographic print of Naiman, Galya Narinskaya, Joseph Brodsky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: variety of typescripts, offprints, manuscripts, photocopies, clippings related to Joseph Brodsky; 1 photograph of Brodsky found in manilla envelope labeled "Бродксий" (Brodsky)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: received in single folder: photographic negatives; manuscript signed "Joseph Brodsky" w/ the heading "Fast-Shrift (sic.) for George L. Kline;" musical score (MSS?) w/ lyrics; unsigned MSS of the poem "Льдины трещали, звенели морозы" by Tatyana Kazanskaya; typescripts of correspondence addressed to Soviet state agencies; MSS letter addressed "Дорогой Иосиф;" newspaper clippings; loose pages from publications; typescript of a poem entitled "Блудный сын"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three 5.25" floppy disks. One includes a description: Naim-Chu.rch.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Attempts to capture disk images, i.e. content, from these disks during 2024 processing were unsuccessful.
